brief.title: Logistics & Supply Chain Performance Dashboard
brief.business_goals: Monitor on-time delivery rates and shipment status
brief.data_context: Data sourced from internal logistics & supply chain systems. Updated monthly. Covers the last 12 months with historical comparison.

recommendation.context_summary: This dashboard serves HR Business Partners in the Logistics & Supply Chain sector. The primary goal is to monitor on-time delivery rates and shipment status. Data is refreshed monthly, and the target users have beginner data literacy. The design should prioritize clarity and actionability over analytical depth.

kpi_task_chart_mapping.rationale: A heatmap reveals patterns in Perfect Order Rate across two dimensions simultaneously.
A gauge chart shows Supplier Lead Time performance relative to a defined threshold or goal.
A scatter plot exposes correlations between Warehouse Utilization and other variables.
A waterfall chart shows how individual components build up to the total Return Rate.

layout_hierarchy.pattern: Executive summary strip at top. Two-column layout: charts left, KPI cards and commentary right.
layout_hierarchy.primary_section: KPI scorecards for Perfect Order Rate, Supplier Lead Time
layout_hierarchy.secondary_section: Trend analysis for Perfect Order Rate over time

labels_scales_colors.color_palette: Financial Navy (#1A237E) with gold (#FFD700) highlights and traffic-light status indicators
labels_scales_colors.axis_labels: Always label axes with units (e.g., Revenue in EUR, Time in Days)
labels_scales_colors.number_format: Percentages: 1 decimal place (e.g., 23.4%). Large numbers: K/M suffix (e.g., 1.2M).
labels_scales_colors.scale_type: Linear for most KPIs; logarithmic if data spans multiple orders of magnitude
labels_scales_colors.legend: Include legend only when comparing 3+ series; use direct labeling otherwise
labels_scales_colors.font_size: Minimum 12px for body text, 16px for KPI values, 20px for dashboard title
labels_scales_colors.accessibility: Ensure 4.5:1 contrast ratio; do not rely on color alone to convey meaning

primary_interactions: Cross-filtering: clicking one chart filters all others
interactions.filter_defaults: Default view: last 30 days, all segments

design_rationales.audience_adaptation: For beginner users: use simple KPI cards and avoid complex charts.
design_rationales.update_frequency_impact: With monthly updates, include a 'last updated' timestamp prominently.
design_rationales.chart_selection_principle: Charts were selected based on the primary user task: trend analysis → line charts; comparison → bar charts; part-to-whole → pie/donut; single value → KPI card.
design_rationales.cognitive_load: Limit to 5-7 visual elements per screen to avoid cognitive overload. Group related KPIs visually using whitespace and section headers.
design_rationales.data_ink_ratio: Remove chart borders, gridlines, and decorative elements. Every pixel should encode data or aid comprehension.

brief.title: Energy & Utilities Performance Dashboard
brief.business_goals: Analyze outage frequency and restoration times
brief.data_context: Data sourced from internal energy & utilities systems. Updated daily. Covers the last 12 months with historical comparison.

recommendation.context_summary: This dashboard serves Product Managers in the Energy & Utilities sector. The primary goal is to analyze outage frequency and restoration times. Data is refreshed daily, and the target users have beginner data literacy. The design should prioritize clarity and actionability over analytical depth.

kpi_task_chart_mapping.rationale: A table provides precise Outage Frequency values for users who need exact numbers.
A KPI card gives immediate visibility into the current Mean Time to Restore value against its target.
A scatter plot exposes correlations between Cost per kWh and other variables.
Donut Chart is appropriate for the task of 'Identify outliers' with Carbon Emissions.

layout_hierarchy.pattern: Single-page scrollable: Summary section → Trend section → Breakdown section → Details.
layout_hierarchy.primary_section: KPI scorecards for Outage Frequency, Mean Time to Restore
layout_hierarchy.secondary_section: Trend analysis for Outage Frequency over time

labels_scales_colors.color_palette: Dark theme: Charcoal (#1E1E1E) with neon green (#00FF88) for positive, red (#FF4444) for negative
labels_scales_colors.axis_labels: Always label axes with units (e.g., Revenue in EUR, Time in Days)
labels_scales_colors.number_format: Currency: 2 decimal places for small values, K/M suffix for large (e.g., €1.2M). Use locale-appropriate separators.
labels_scales_colors.scale_type: Linear for most KPIs; logarithmic if data spans multiple orders of magnitude
labels_scales_colors.legend: Include legend only when comparing 3+ series; use direct labeling otherwise
labels_scales_colors.font_size: Minimum 12px for body text, 16px for KPI values, 20px for dashboard title
labels_scales_colors.accessibility: Ensure 4.5:1 contrast ratio; do not rely on color alone to convey meaning

primary_interactions: Zoom and pan on time-series charts
interactions.filter_defaults: Default view: last 30 days, all segments

design_rationales.audience_adaptation: For beginner users: use simple KPI cards and avoid complex charts.
design_rationales.update_frequency_impact: With daily updates, include a 'last updated' timestamp prominently.
design_rationales.chart_selection_principle: Charts were selected based on the primary user task: trend analysis → line charts; comparison → bar charts; part-to-whole → pie/donut; single value → KPI card.
design_rationales.cognitive_load: Limit to 5-7 visual elements per screen to avoid cognitive overload. Group related KPIs visually using whitespace and section headers.
design_rationales.data_ink_ratio: Remove chart borders, gridlines, and decorative elements. Every pixel should encode data or aid comprehension.

brief.title: Retail Performance Dashboard
brief.business_goals: Monitor store sales performance by region
brief.data_context: Data sourced from internal retail systems. Updated real-time. Covers the last 12 months with historical comparison.

recommendation.context_summary: This dashboard serves C-Level Executives (CEO, CFO, COO) in the Retail sector. The primary goal is to monitor store sales performance by region. Data is refreshed real-time, and the target users have advanced / expert data literacy. The design should prioritize clarity and actionability over analytical depth.

kpi_task_chart_mapping.rationale: Stacked Bar Chart is appropriate for the task of 'Monitor trend over time' with Average Transaction Value.
A scatter plot exposes correlations between Stock Availability and other variables.
Area Chart is appropriate for the task of 'Track against target' with Sell-Through Rate.
A funnel chart visualizes the conversion steps contributing to Return Rate.

layout_hierarchy.pattern: Grid layout: 12-column responsive grid. Hero metric top-left, supporting charts fill remaining space.
layout_hierarchy.primary_section: KPI scorecards for Average Transaction Value, Stock Availability
layout_hierarchy.secondary_section: Trend analysis for Average Transaction Value over time
